Video.summary: Welcome to The Fly Shop Tour! In September of 2022, we set across the Rocky Mountains on a 2500 Mile Road Trip visiting & fishing with local Fly Shops. Our Mission? To highlight small businesses that drive the sport we love: Fly Fishing! Along the way, we got a chance to host meetups in every state - all with the goal to raise money for local conservation organizations & meet some of our fans. Follow us as we tell the story of 12 unique fly shops, fish the waters surrounding their area, and make our way throughout the Rocky Mountains.
Video.title: "Our 2500 Mile ROAD TRIP Begins"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair heads to the great state of Alaska to bring you a sight never seen before on this show, Capt. Blair in close-toed shoes. He catches some petty cool fish too, thanks to Capt. Geoff Peck at Baranof Fishing Excursions.
Video.title: "Father's Day Bucket List"

Video.summary: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.
Video.title: Wildbound - "Alabama"

Video.summary: 82 year old Navy veteran with more than 2.7 million TikTok followers, Patriotic Kenny talks of how he helps other vets through him gifting mobility scooters to fellow veterans in need.
Video.title: "Patriotic Kenny"

Video.summary: After multiple days in Alaska, it was time for the last trip out to sea. The crew got to experience the sights on this trip, like God Valley and others. And of course catch more Alaskan fish.
Video.title: "The Final Voyage"

Video.summary: Mike heads to Crystal river to chase Redfish in his new Cayo Skiff.
Video.title: "Kerr Bar"

Video.summary: Mike tries to hold his own while fishing with Nick Fischer and Liza Fitzgerald in south seas Captiva. Good luck buddy.
Video.title: "Lil Jon"

Video.summary: Mike heads to the plantation on Crystal River to fish with Dallas King for trout and redfish.
Video.title: "King of the Mud Minnow"

Video.summary: Part one of Mike and Beth's adventure to Costa Rica's Zancudo Lodge.
Video.title: "Mrs Anderson's Rooster"

Video.summary: Part two of the Zancudo adventure. Mike and Beth fish for Mahi, Sailfish and Cubera Snapper.
Video.title: "(Costa Rica part 2) "Big Fish Javier""

Video.summary: Dan Cabela sets out to learn about the model of private ownership of wildlife in South Africa. Along the way, he participates in game capture, attends an auction and hunts to support a community.
Video.title: "Private Ownership of Wildlife"

Video.summary: Dan Cabela dives into the heart of one of the world’s largest game reserves - the Selous. He explores the synergy between hunting and conservation that is preserving this wild paradise.
Video.title: "A Conservation Legacy"

Video.summary: Join Dan Cabela in New Zealand's breathtaking landscapes as he hunts non-native species, delving into the crucial role of controlled harvesting and the complexities of managing introduced wildlife.
Video.title: "Hunting in the Kiwi Wilds"

Video.summary: Explore Pakistan's wilderness with Dan Cabela as he treks the rugged mountains, in search of ibex and urial, and learns about the community conservation model that has protected these species.
Video.title: "Community Conservation in Pakistan"

Video.summary: Capt. Blair has some down time in Alaska, so what does he do? Heads to the beach to check out the salmon hatchery in Ketchican. They've met their quota for the year so the season is open, but the trick is how to catch the salmon. There hasn't been an episode like this yet.
Video.title: "Salmon Snagging"

Video.summary: When the captain calls and says the weather in Alaska is great, you don't waste the opportunity. So Capt. Blair and the whole crew head out for a day of fishing. With one camera on the boat, things got a little chaotic with all the catching going on.
Video.title: "Weather Window Day"

Video.summary: Capt Justin & Meredith’s beautiful Panama City Beach morning is met with a slack tide so a new path to redfish success is found under a popular bridge.
Video.title: "A Bridge Too Red"

Video.summary: If you had a blank check to build the best fishing boat possible, what would you create? Capt. Justin gets his wish, but it doesn’t come quickly or easily.
Video.title: "Dream Boat Build"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin takes a fellow guide on his new boat to see how it measures up. Using only public waypoints on his Humminbird unit, they find exploring to be as much fun as it is fruitful.
Video.title: "Fishing the Dream Boat"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin heads offshore, dodges storms and shows how the "Return 'Em Right" program is saving fish that experience barotrauma.
Video.title: "Happy Returns"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Florida Keys guide Jeff Malone to find a productive wreck with which they enjoy the exciting variety of fish that wrecks can provide.
Video.title: "Jeff Malones New Wreck"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by marine scientist Nick Castillo to search for bonefish in Key Largo for both fun and study.
Video.title: "Bonefish Science With Nick Castillo"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor take a trip to the Marathon, FL area to challenge some feisty sharks and sneaky snook.
Video.title: "Sharks And Snook Near Home"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by young guide Richard Black as he leads them to a backcountry slam.
Video.title: "Richard Black Slam"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by young up and coming guide Anthony Vargas, who puts them on a wide variety of game fish in the Keys backcountry.
Video.title: "Anthony Guides For The Win"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Key West guide Nick LaBadie, a talented guide with a special knack for finding permit on the Key West flats.
Video.title: "Key West with Nick Labadie"

Video.summary: Huckberry's flagship adventure series is back in Japan and the DIRT crew is hungry as ever for adventure. This time, they’re diving into the Kansai region—where millennia-old traditions flow from the shore of Lake Biwa to surf breaks on the coast. Chef Coleman Griffin gives them a taste of the deep cultural immersion that inspires his culinary practice, from chef knives descended from samurai swords to fermented carp that went on to spawn sushi. And turns out, the perfect meal does exist, and the crew knew it as soon as they had a bite of their final omakase.
Video.title: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger join Capt. Brain Wilson in Galveston, TX for some hot Red Snapper action.
Video.title: "Red Snapper Slam"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger go north for a change to sight fish tripletails in Florida Bay.
Video.title: "Triple Tail in the Florida Bay"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger head to the hump on a windy day to target tunas and find some bonus mahis and tripletails as well.
Video.title: "Rough Day at the Hump"

Video.summary: Being consistently productive on the water means knowing how to adapt to conditions. Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger switch it up and fish shallow for sailfish.
Video.title: "Sailfish Save The Day"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger launch from the incomparable Oceans Edge Resort in Key West, Florida to hunt down all kinds of sport fish.
Video.title: "Key West Mahi and Sails"

Video.summary: U.S. Coast Guard helicopter pilot Shay Williams recounts his career flying countless search and rescue missions.
Video.title: "Shay Williams"

Video.summary: An Honor No One Wants: A Gold Star Family is the immediate family member(s) of a fallen soldier who died while serving in a time of conflict. Sheila Mitchell-Murphy honors the memory her Son…American hero Etienne Murphy.
Video.title: "Sheila Mitchell-Murphy"

Video.summary: U.S. Army veteran Domingo Martinez recounts overcoming the life altering effects of trauma he received as a child and on the DMZ.
Video.title: "Domingo Martinez"

Video.summary: Trenton stays close to home and heads to the Lake of The Ozarks joining Austin Culbertson for a frigid couple days of fishing. The cold weather doesn’t hold back the fish, though, as they target brush piles with jerk baits and umbrella rigs.
Video.title: “Ozark Winter”

Video.summary: Heavy hearts find comfort in cherished memories as the Local Knowledge crew mourns the loss of one of the greatest members of the fishing industry. Looking back on several of our episodes with our dear friend Dave Salazar, we celebrate the life of this amazing fishermen, family man, and visionary behind the world renowned Casa Vieja Lodge.
Video.title: "El Jefe"

Video.summary: The ability to overcome obstacles and challenging conditions can be key to success, but when fishing in Key West, cold temps are rarely the hurdle. For Rush and Ali, it's another day on the water where patience and a plan pays off with Cobia and Amberjack action that gets the rods bent after a cold weather spell in the Keys.
Video.title: "Cobia So Cold"

Video.summary: When it comes to fishing in Key West, you can often get a lot done in a little bit of time. After an early check in to Ocean’s Edge Resort and a mid day departure from then dock, Ali looks to Rush to put him on an afternoon bite of tuna and bottom fish that few other places can offer. If Rush is going to deliver, he will need to make a plan and stick to it to make the best use of this limited time.
Video.title: "Afternoon Delight"

Video.summary: For Ali and Rush, one of the best aspects of fishing is being able to share experiences on the water with interesting people. On this episode we invite luxury watch enthusiast Eric Rivera - AKA "Watch Eric" to the Florida Keys to target Permit and Amberjack in the Gulf.
Video.title: "Watch Eric"

Video.summary: The Odyssea Sportfishing family grows as Casey Villone comes aboard as Rush's new deck hand. While Rush gained a helping hand on his charters, he also scored a new "Fishing Mate" who is always game to bend a rod. With a day off and a report of Tuna offshore, the two head out to see if they can beat the sharks and land a Key West sushi dinner.
Video.title: "Fishing Mate"

Video.summary: Rush teams up with longtime friend and fellow angler Jesus Diaz for a well-earned "busman's holiday" in the beautiful waters of Key West. Even on a day off, these two can't resist seeing what's going to bite. Taking the wind and the tides into account, the duo maps out a strategic circuit through Key West's surrounding waters to target cobia, grouper, snapper, and tuna for a busy day "off" work.
Video.title: "Fishing With Zeus in Key West"

Video.summary: Water quality is everything—and the Star brite - SeaSafe team is back at it for the second year in a row! Led by Cory Redwine, Conservation Director of Project SeaSafe, this is the 4th Annual Clean Water Collective and it’s bigger than ever. This time, we’re in Grant, Florida, where 4 million clams are being released into the Indian River Lagoon, paired with the planting of thousands of mangroves, oyster work by CCA and trash clean ups to help restore one of Florida’s most critical ecosystems. But it’s not just about hard work—it’s about connection. With local captains, fishing legends, and the Local Knowledge crew on hand, we hit the water to show why this place is worth fighting for. From clam drops by drone, to in-water mangrove planting, to monster snook catches, this is hands-on conservation at its best. This two-part series kicks off with action, heart, and the kind of hope only a healthy waterway can bring. Don’t miss Part 1—where conservation meets community and everyone gets their hands wet.
Video.title: "SeaSafe Snook Part 1"

Video.summary: As part of this year's Star brite Sea Safe event, we hit the water to experience some of the incredible fishing the Indian River Lagoon has to offer. For Rush, that meant some serious bow time with close friend Alex Hughey - on the hunt for some of the biggest snook we've ever encountered. The bite wasn't easy, but that didn't stop Rush from smashing his personal best - not once, but twice - in one unforgettable day on the water. Don't miss this action-packed episode of the Sea Safe adventure from beautiful Sebastian, Florida!
Video.title: "SeaSafe Snook Part 2"

Video.summary: SoCal summer is calling, and the Local Knowledge crew is back on Ali’s home turf in San Diego, California. With reports of hot bites and fish moving in, the crew faces a classic Southern California dilemma — chase big yellowtail around the Coronado Islands or head north in search of bluefin tuna. The options are plenty, the challenges are real, and that’s exactly what keeps fishing in SoCal so exciting.
Video.title: "Dog Gone Yellow"

Video.summary: We will take you to some of the wrecks in the Florida Keys that locals love to dive and share their rich history and the stories held deep aboard these sunken ships. We will also take a deep dive and discuss how these wrecks are critical to the environment.
Video.title: "Wrecks - Where Locals Love to Dive"

Video.summary: The Kampar River in Sumatra, Indonesia boasts the world’s longest wave: a cyclical tidal bore known as “Bono Seven Ghosts” that can run over 13 miles long and be surfed for an hour-and-a-half. This Rivers are Life adventure film explores the relationship between the Kampar River and its surrounding communities, focusing on how surfing has strengthened this connection.
Video.title: "Chasing The Bono"

Video.summary: This episode of Huckberry Homes brings us out to the Western Slope, just a few miles outside of Vail, Colorado to visit our longtime friend and collaborator Sinuhe Xavier. When he's not traveling the world shooting commercials and making short films, Xavier's at home in his quiet, perfectly appointed retreat deep in the Rockies here he's a three hour drive from Red Rock desert and a ten minute drive from some of the best snowboarding in the world. There was plenty of magic in Sinuhe's spot, from his BMW GS to his exploration of how his love for his son shaped the design and decoration of his pad.
Video.title: "Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"

Image.alt: "Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"
"Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"
"Inside This Adventure Filmmaker's Gear Garage & Colorado Home | Sinuhe Xavier"

Video.summary: The subject of multiple Vincent Van Gogh paintings, the Dommel River is the backbone of one of the ’smartest’ and most economically valuable regions in the Netherlands. Known as the ‘Brainport Region’ – a high-tech business ecosystem akin to Silicon Valley – the unique area, centered around Eindhoven, relies on urban and industrial development for continued economic prosperity. However, development has recently come to a halt, guided by strict EU environmental targets: the Dommel River basin – once abundant with wetlands – must be fixed, and specific bio-diversity levels met, before any further development is to take place in Eindhoven.
Video.title: "Portrait of a River"
